message:
fix: widen non-timeout test case timeouts to prevent flakiness (gt-z5je)

The "non-zero exit with stdout" subtest of TestRunBdCmd and the success
case of TestRunCmd used tight timeouts (2s/500ms) despite not testing
timeout behavior. Under CI load, process startup alone can take >1s
(observed 1.78s-5.89s for a trivial echo+exit script), causing
intermittent false timeout failures.

Increase to 30s for both non-timeout test paths. The timeout subtests
retain their short (200ms) deadlines since they specifically test
timeout behavior against a 10s sleep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
